name: Indonesia

colors.symbolism: courage; the body and the physical world
purity; the soul and the spiritual world

description: Two equal horizontal stripes — red over white.
history: Hoisted by Sukarno on 17 August 1945 at the proclamation of Indonesian independence and formally adopted on 18 August 1945 in the 1945 Constitution. The red-and-white colours have been associated with the Indonesian archipelago since the 13th-century Majapahit empire and were adopted by the Indonesian nationalist movement in the early 20th century. The flag is virtually identical in design to the flag of Monaco, which is taller (4:5) but uses the same colours and order; the flag of Poland uses the same stripes reversed.

features.symbolism: the Sang Saka Merah-Putih (Sacred Red-and-White) — red for courage and the body, white for purity and the soul; together the unity of body and soul
